LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACCAR INC'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S SHOWING IS CONSISTENT WITH THE HOLDING IN MORRIS V. AGFA (2006) 144 CAL. APP. 4TH 1452. CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM. MOST, IF NOT ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS MAY BE SUED IN ALABAMA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-23-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Continued To Nov-19-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Paccar Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Dec-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACCAR INC'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS

Appearances:
Angel Lewis of Buty & Curliano (510.267.3000) for defendant and moving party Paccar, Inc.
Pegah Shetabi of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808.0300) for defendant and joining party Foster Wheeler, LLC
James Nevin of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion to stay is granted. Defendant's showing is consistent with the holding in Morris v. AGFA (2006) 144 Cal. App. 4th 1452. California is a seriously inconvenient forum. Most, if not all of the defendants may be sued in Alabama. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Joinder To Deft Paccar Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Action On The Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens
